creator: bugs-freebsd-org916@ketas.si.pri.ee
text:
i don't know what's going on there

yes, host which has usb serial attached is 13.4. and it feels like those things just silently corrupt my kernel memory, not just themselves

i don't know where the issue is, ucom, etc. usb. usb serial drivers

i have all sorts of adapters, isolated and unisolated. all cheap tho. pl, cp, ftdi (ft232r, ft232h). cp2102 particular is nice because it's cheap, it's available, and has eeprom for own serial

there is gps connected too here, strangely suddenly nmea got corrupted. this is on pl and is streaming all the time. maybe pl or gps lost it royally

but:

gpsd refused to recognize it suddenly and i went to look

the hw i run could be bad, but it can't be that all of it is bad? and the more i push it, the worse it gets? everyones every usb serial is bad on lot of traffic and it mixes up input and output when you open the port?

i don't know where the issue is really. unsure about kernel list. people on irc agreed that something fishy is going on. it's also a bug that doesn't always appear. since how many people do this. or maybe they do. anyway i tested it and something fails 100% now if i push it

sadly i don't likely find the real culprit on my own
